children.heading: Amount for grants
children.label: 1 Amount for grants
children.text: Not more than $3,000,000 shall be distributed to the Secretary of the Interior for making multistate conservation project grants in accordance with this section.

children.heading: Period of availability
children.label: A Period of availability
children.text: Amounts made available under paragraph (1) shall remain available for making grants only for the first fiscal year for which the amount is made available and the following fiscal year.

children.heading: Apportionment
children.label: B Apportionment
children.text: At the end of the period of availability under subparagraph (A), the Secretary of the Interior shall apportion any amounts that remain available among the States in the manner specified in for use by the States in the same manner as funds apportioned under .section 777c(c) of this titlesection 777c(c) of this title

children.heading: States or entities to be benefited
children.label: 1 States or entities to be benefited
A
children.text: at least 26 States;

children.label: B
children.text: a majority of the States in a region of the United States Fish and Wildlife Service; or

children.label: C
children.text: a regional association of State fish and game departments.

children.heading: Use of submitted priority list of projects
children.label: 2 Use of submitted priority list of projects
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior may make grants under this section only for projects identified on a priority list of sport fish restoration projects described in paragraph (3).

children.label: i
children.text: nongovernmental organizations that represent conservation organizations;

children.label: ii
children.text: sportsmen organizations; and

children.label: iii
children.text: industries that fund the sport fish restoration programs under this chapter;

children.label: B
children.text: approves by vote of a majority of the heads of State fish and game departments (or their designees); and

children.label: C
children.text: not later than October 1 of each fiscal year, submits to the Assistant Director for Wildlife and Sport Fish Restoration Programs.

children.heading: Publication
children.label: 4 Publication
children.text: The Assistant Director for Wildlife and Sport Fish Restoration Programs shall publish in the Federal Register each priority list submitted under paragraph (3)(C).

children.label: A
children.text: a State or group of States;

children.label: B
children.text: the United States Fish and Wildlife Service, or a State or group of States, for the purpose of carrying out the National Survey of Fishing, Hunting, and Wildlife-Associated Recreation; and

children.label: C
children.text: subject to paragraph (2), a nongovernmental organization.

children.label: i
children.text: will not use the grant funds to fund, in whole or in part, any activity of the organization that promotes or encourages opposition to the regulated taking of fish; and

children.label: ii
children.text: will use the grant funds in compliance with subsection (d).

children.heading: Penalties for certain activities
children.label: B Penalties for certain activities
children.text: Any nongovernmental organization that is found to use grant funds in violation of subparagraph (A) shall return all funds received under this section and be subject to any other applicable penalties under law.

children.heading: Use of grants
children.label: d Use of grants
children.text: A grant under this section shall not be used, in whole or in part, for an activity, project, or program that promotes or encourages opposition to the regulated taking of fish.

children.label: A
children.text: the Atlantic States Marine Fisheries Commission;

children.label: B
children.text: the Gulf States Marine Fisheries Commission;

children.label: C
children.text: the Pacific States Marine Fisheries Commission; and

children.label: D
children.text: the Great Lakes Fisheries Commission.
$400,000 shall be made available for the Sport Fishing and Boating Partnership Council established by the United States Fish and Wildlife Service.

A portion, as determined by the Sport Fishing and Boating Partnership Council, of funds disbursed for the purposes described in paragraph (2) but remaining unobligated as of , shall be used to study the impact of derelict vessels and identify recyclable solutions for recreational vessels.October 1, 2021

children.heading: Nonapplicability of chapter 10 of title 5
children.label: f Nonapplicability of chapter 10 of title 5
children.text: Chapter 10 of title 5 shall not apply to any activity carried out under this section.
